claims.text: We're live from Georgia, a key battleground state in the race for the White House in just moments the current U.S. president will debate the former U.S. president as their party's presumptive nominees of first in American history.
claims.claim: First debate between current and former U.S. presidents as party nominees
claims.source: U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

claims.text: We want to welcome our viewers in the United States and around the world to our studios in Atlanta.
claims.claim: Welcome viewers in the United States and around the world to Atlanta studios
claims.source: U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

claims.text: This is the CNN presidential debate.
claims.claim: This is the CNN presidential debate

claims.text: This debate is being produced by CNN and is coming to you live on CNN, CNN International, CNN.com, CNN Max and CNN Español.
claims.claim: This debate is produced by CNN and broadcast on CNN platforms

claims.text: This is a pivotal moment between President Joe Biden and former President Donald Trump in their rematch for the nation's highest office.
claims.claim: This is a pivotal moment between Biden and Trump in their rematch

claims.text: Each will make his case to the American people with just over four months until election day.
claims.claim: Each will make his case to the American people with just over four months until election day
claims.source: U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

claims.text: Good evening, I'm Dana Bash, anchor of CNN's inside politics and co-anchor of State of the Union.
claims.claim: Dana Bash is anchor of CNN's Inside Politics and co-anchor of State of the Union

claims.text: I'm Jake Tapper, anchor of CNN's the lead and co-anchor of State of the Union.
claims.claim: Jake Tapper is anchor of CNN's The Lead and co-anchor of State of the Union

claims.text: Dana and I will co-moderate this evening.
claims.claim: Dana and I will co-moderate this evening

claims.text: Our job is to facilitate a debate between the two candidates tonight.
claims.claim: Our job is to facilitate a debate between the two candidates tonight.

claims.text: Before we introduce them, we want to share the rules of the debate with the audience at home.
claims.claim: Sharing debate rules with the audience at home

claims.text: Former President Trump will be on the left side of the screen.
claims.claim: Former President Trump will be on the left side of the screen

claims.text: President Biden will be appearing on the right, a coin toss, determined their positions.
claims.claim: President Biden will be appearing on the right, a coin toss, determined their positions

claims.text: Each candidate will have two minutes to answer a question and one minute each for responses and rebuttals.
claims.claim: Each candidate will have two minutes to answer and one minute for responses

claims.text: An additional minute for follow-up clarification or response is at the moderator's discretion.
claims.claim: An additional minute for follow-up is at the moderator's discretion
claims.source: Joint Committee on Taxation / Committee for a Responsible Federal Budget

claims.text: When it's time for a candidate to speak, his microphone will be turned on and his opponent's microphone will be turned off.
claims.claim: Candidate's microphone is turned on and opponent's turned off when speaking

claims.text: Should a candidate interrupt when his microphone is muted, he will be difficult to understand for viewers at home.
claims.claim: A candidate interrupting when muted is hard to understand for viewers

claims.text: At the end of the debate, each candidate will get two minutes for closing statements.
claims.claim: Each candidate gets two minutes for closing statements

claims.text: There is no studio audience tonight.
claims.claim: There is no studio audience tonight
claims.source: U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

claims.text: Free-written notes, props or contact with campaign staff are not permitted during the debate.
claims.claim: Free-written notes, props or contact with campaign staff are not permitted during the debate

claims.text: By accepting our invitation to debate both candidates and their campaigns agreed to accept these rules.
claims.claim: Both candidates agreed to accept debate rules by accepting invitation

claims.text: Now, please welcome the 46th President of the United States, Joe Biden, and please welcome the 45th President of the United States, Donald Trump.
claims.claim: Joe Biden is the 46th and Donald Trump the 45th U.S. Presidents

claims.text: Gentlemen, thanks so much for being here.
claims.claim: Gentlemen, thanks so much for being here.

claims.text: Let's begin the debate and let's start with the issue that voters consistently say, is their top concern, the economy.
claims.claim: Voters consistently say the economy is their top concern

claims.text: President Biden, inflation has slowed, but prices remain high.
claims.claim: Inflation has slowed but prices remain high

claims.text: Since you took office, the price of essentials has increased.
claims.claim: Since you took office, the price of essentials has increased

claims.text: For example, a basket of groceries that cost $100 then now costs more than $120.
claims.claim: A basket of groceries that cost $100 now costs more than $120
claims.correction: Grocery prices rose about 21% from January 2021 to mid-2024

claims.text: And typical home prices have jumped more than 30%.
claims.claim: Typical home prices have jumped more than 30%

claims.text: What do you say to voters who feel they are worse off under your presidency than they were under President Trump?
claims.claim: Voters feel worse off under current presidency than under Trump

claims.text: We got to take a look at what I was left when I became president and what Mr. Trump left me.
claims.claim: Trump left office with fewer jobs than when he started

claims.text: We had an economy that was in free fall.
claims.claim: The economy was in free fall.
claims.correction: Before COVID, real GDP growth averaged about 2.5% per year, indicating a strong economy.

claims.text: The pandemic was so badly handled.
claims.claim: The pandemic was so badly handled

claims.text: Many people were dying.
claims.claim: Many people were dying
claims.source: U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

claims.text: All he said was, it's not that serious, just injected a bleach into your arm, it could be all right.
claims.claim: He said injecting bleach could be all right
claims.correction: Trump mused about injecting disinfectant, not bleach verbatim, on April 23, 2020

claims.text: The economy collapsed.
claims.claim: The economy collapsed.
claims.correction: Economy lost 2.7 million jobs over Trump's term due to COVID-19, but pre-pandemic growth was solid at 2.5% annually.

claims.text: There were no jobs.
claims.claim: There were no jobs
claims.correction: The economy lost 2.7 million jobs under Trump, but manufacturing jobs rebounded by 700,000–800,000 under Biden.

claims.text: Unemployment rate rose to 15%.
claims.claim: Unemployment rate rose to 15%
claims.correction: Unemployment peaked at 14.7% in April 2020, not 15%

claims.text: It was terrible.
claims.claim: It was terrible
claims.source: Joint Committee on Taxation / Committee for a Responsible Federal Budget

claims.text: And so what we had to do was try to put things back together again.
claims.claim: We had to try to put things back together again

claims.text: And that's exactly what we began to do.
claims.claim: And that's exactly what we began to do.

claims.text: We created 15,000 new jobs.
claims.claim: We created 15,000 new jobs.
claims.correction: The U.S. economy added roughly 15–16 million jobs under Biden, not 15,000.

claims.text: And we brought out a position where we have 800,000 new manufacturing jobs, but there's more to be done.
claims.claim: 800,000 new manufacturing jobs were created

claims.text: There's more to be done.
claims.claim: There's more to be done.

claims.text: Working-class people are still in trouble.
claims.claim: Working-class people are still in trouble

claims.text: I come from Scranton, Pennsylvania.
claims.claim: I come from Scranton, Pennsylvania.

claims.text: I come up household.
claims.claim: I come up household

claims.text: We're at a kitchen table of the things we're unable to be met during the month.
claims.claim: We're at a kitchen table of the things we're unable to be met during the month

claims.text: It was a problem.
claims.claim: It was a problem.
claims.source: Joint Committee on Taxation / Committee for a Responsible Federal Budget

claims.text: The price of eggs, the price of gas, the price of housing, the price of a whole range of things.
claims.claim: Prices of eggs, gas, housing, and many things increased

claims.text: That's why I'm working so hard to make sure I deal with those problems.
claims.claim: Working hard to deal with problems

claims.text: And we're going to make sure that we have reduced the price of housing.
claims.claim: We have reduced the price of housing
claims.correction: National home prices rose more than 30% from January 2021 to mid-2024

claims.text: We're going to make sure we built two million new units.
claims.claim: We will build two million new units

claims.text: We're going to make sure we cap rents so corporate greed can't take over.
claims.claim: We will cap rents to prevent corporate greed
claims.correction: No reference fact confirms any rent cap policy or legislation

claims.text: The combination that I was left with in corporate greed is the reason why we're doing this problem right now.
claims.claim: Corporate greed caused the current problem

claims.text: In addition to that, we're in a situation where if you take a look at all that was done in his administration, he didn't do much at all.
claims.claim: Trump didn't do much during his administration
claims.correction: Trump's term saw a $7.8 trillion debt rise and record $3.1 trillion deficit in 2020

claims.text: By the time he left, there were things that were in chaos, literally chaos.
claims.claim: By the time he left, there were things that were in chaos
claims.correction: Over 400,000 Americans died of COVID-19; economy lost 2.7 million jobs by January 2021
claims.source: U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

claims.text: And so we put things back together.
claims.claim: And so we put things back together.

claims.text: We created, I said, those jobs, we make sure we had a situation where we now brought down the price of prescription drugs, which is a major issue for many people.
claims.claim: We created jobs and brought down prescription drug prices

claims.text: If it's a $15 for an insulin shot, that's supposed to $400, no senior has to pay more than $200 for any drug, all the drugs they can include beginning next year.
claims.claim: No senior pays more than $200 for any drug starting next year
claims.correction: Insulin capped at $35/month; total annual out-of-pocket drug costs capped at $2,000 starting 2025

claims.text: And the situation is making, we're going to make that available to everybody, to all Americans.
claims.claim: making that available to all Americans
claims.source: U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

claims.text: So we're working to bring down the price of it from the kitchen table, and that's what we're going to get done.
claims.claim: working to bring down the price of it from the kitchen table

claims.text: Thank you.
claims.claim: Thank you.

claims.text: President Trump?
claims.claim: President Trump

claims.text: We are the greatest economy in the history of our country.
claims.claim: We are the greatest economy in the history of our country
claims.correction: Pre-pandemic GDP growth was solid but not historically exceptional; late 1990s growth was higher

claims.text: We have never done so well.
claims.claim: We have never done so well
claims.correction: The economy lost 2.7 million jobs during Trump's term; over 400,000 COVID deaths by Jan 2021

claims.text: Everybody was amazed by it.
claims.claim: Everybody was amazed by it

claims.text: Other countries were copying us.
claims.claim: Other countries were copying us

claims.text: We got hit with COVID.
claims.claim: We got hit with COVID
claims.source: U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

claims.text: And when we did, we spent the money necessary.
claims.claim: We spent the money necessary during the pandemic
claims.source: U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

claims.text: So we wouldn't end up in a great depression, the likes of which we had in 1929.
claims.claim: Avoided a Great Depression like 1929

claims.text: By the time we finished, so we did a great job.
claims.claim: By the time we finished, so we did a great job.

claims.text: We got a lot of credit for the economy, a lot of credit for the military and no wars and so many other things.
claims.claim: We got a lot of credit for the economy, military, no wars, and other things
claims.correction: Economy growth was solid but not exceptional; Trump left office with 2.7 million fewer jobs; no wars claim unverified

claims.text: Everything was rocking good.
claims.claim: Everything was rocking good

claims.text: But the thing we never got the credit for, and we should have, is getting us out of that COVID mess.
claims.claim: We got credit for getting us out of the COVID mess
claims.correction: Over 400,000 Americans died by Jan 2021; early response drew significant criticism
claims.source: U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

claims.text: He created mandates that was a disaster for our country.
claims.claim: He created mandates that was a disaster for our country
claims.correction: Trump's term saw 2.7 million net job losses and 400,000+ COVID deaths; mandates' impact is debated

claims.text: Other than that, we had given them back a country where the stock market actually was higher than pre-COVID, and nobody thought that was even possible.
claims.claim: Stock market was higher than pre-COVID when Trump left office

claims.text: The only jobs he created are for illegal immigrants and bounce back jobs, a bounce back from the COVID.
claims.claim: Only jobs created are for illegal immigrants and bounce back jobs
claims.correction: Most job gains under Biden were for native-born workers, not only unauthorized immigrants

claims.text: He has not done a good job.
claims.claim: He has not done a good job.

claims.text: He's done a poor job in inflation's killing our country.
claims.claim: He's done a poor job in inflation's killing our country
claims.correction: Inflation peaked at 9.1% in June 2022 but declined to about 3.0–3.3% by mid-2024
claims.source: U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics CPI

claims.text: It is absolutely killing us.
claims.claim: It is absolutely killing us.

claims.text: Thank you.
claims.claim: Thank you.

claims.text: President Biden?
claims.claim: President Biden?

claims.text: Well, look, the greatest economy in the world, he's the only one who thinks that, I think.
claims.claim: Only he thinks the economy is the greatest in the world

claims.text: He's the greatest, the greatest economy in the world.
claims.claim: He's the greatest, the greatest economy in the world
claims.correction: Real GDP growth under Trump averaged 2.5% yearly, solid but not historically exceptional

claims.text: And the fact of the matter is that we prun ourselves in a situation where his economy, he rewarded the wealthy.
claims.claim: Trump's economy rewarded the wealthy
claims.correction: Trump's economy had 2.5% GDP growth but lost 2.7 million jobs over term

claims.text: He had the largest tax cut in American history, $2 trillion.
claims.claim: He had the largest tax cut in American history, $2 trillion
claims.correction: 2017 tax cut was about $1.5 trillion over 10 years, not $2 trillion; Reagan's 1981 cut was larger
claims.source: Joint Committee on Taxation / Committee for a Responsible Federal Budget

claims.text: He raised a deficit larger than he president has in any one term.
claims.claim: He raised a deficit larger than any president in one term

claims.text: He's the only president other than Herbert Hoover, who's had lost more jobs than he had when he began, since Herbert Hoover, the idea that he did something that was significant and the military.
claims.claim: Only Trump and Hoover lost jobs since Hoover

claims.text: You know, when he was president, there were still killing people in Afghanistan.
claims.claim: People were still being killed in Afghanistan during Trump's presidency

claims.text: He didn't do anything about that.
claims.claim: He didn't do anything about that

claims.text: When he was president, we were still finding ourselves in a position where you had a notion that we were this safe country.
claims.claim: We thought we were a safe country during his presidency

claims.text: Truth is, I'm the only president this century that doesn't have any, this decade, that no, any troops dying anywhere in the world like he did.
claims.claim: No troops dying anywhere in this decade under this president
claims.source: U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

claims.text: President Trump, I want to follow up if I can.
claims.claim: President Trump, I want to follow up if I can.

claims.text: You won't have to respond to him.
claims.claim: You won't have to respond to him.

claims.text: Well, I'm going to ask you a follow up.
claims.claim: I'm going to ask you a follow up

claims.text: You can do whatever you want with the minute that we give you.
claims.claim: You can do whatever you want with the minute that we give you.

claims.text: I want to follow up.
claims.claim: I want to follow up.
claims.source: U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

claims.text: You want to impose a 10% tariff on all goods coming into the U.S.?
claims.claim: You want to impose a 10% tariff on all goods coming into the U.S.

claims.text: How will you ensure that that doesn't drive prices even higher?
claims.claim: Higher tariffs will drive prices even higher
claims.correction: A broad 10% import tariff tends to raise U.S. consumer prices (Federal Reserve research)

claims.text: I'm not going to drive them higher.
claims.claim: I'm not going to drive them higher

claims.text: It's just going to cause countries that have been ripping us off for years like China and many others in all fairness to China.
claims.claim: Countries like China have been ripping us off for years
claims.correction: U.S. tariffs are paid by American importers and consumers, not foreign countries like China
claims.source: Joint Committee on Taxation / Committee for a Responsible Federal Budget

claims.text: It's going to just force them to pay us a lot of money, reduce our deficit tremendously and give us a lot of power for other things.
claims.claim: It will force them to pay us money, reduce deficit, and increase power

claims.text: But he made a statement.
claims.claim: He made a statement

claims.text: The only thing he was right about is I gave you the largest tax cut in history.
claims.claim: He gave the largest tax cut in history
claims.correction: The 2017 tax cut was about $1.5 trillion over 10 years, smaller than Reagan's 1981 cut
claims.source: Joint Committee on Taxation / Committee for a Responsible Federal Budget

claims.text: I also gave you the largest regulation cut in history.
claims.claim: I gave the largest regulation cut in history
claims.correction: 2017 tax cuts were $1.5 trillion over 10 years, smaller than Reagan's 1981 cut
claims.source: Joint Committee on Taxation / Committee for a Responsible Federal Budget

claims.text: That's why we had all the jobs.
claims.claim: We had all the jobs

claims.text: And the jobs went down and then they bounced back and he's taking credit for bounced back jobs.
claims.claim: Jobs went down then bounced back and he takes credit for bounced back jobs

claims.text: You can't do that.
claims.claim: You can't do that.

claims.text: He also said he inherited 9% inflation.
claims.claim: He inherited 9% inflation
claims.correction: U.S. inflation peaked at about 9.1% in June 2022 when he took office
claims.source: U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics CPI

claims.text: Now, he inherited almost no inflation and it stayed that way for 14 months.
claims.claim: He inherited almost no inflation
claims.correction: Inflation peaked at about 9.1% in June 2022, indicating high inflation at inheritance
claims.source: U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics CPI

claims.text: And then it blew up under his leadership because they spent money like a bunch of people that didn't know what they were doing.
claims.claim: Economy blew up under his leadership due to reckless spending
claims.correction: Economy lost 2.7 million jobs mainly due to COVID-19; S&P 500 rose 67% during Trump term

claims.text: And they don't know what they were doing.
claims.claim: They don't know what they were doing

claims.text: It was probably the worst administration in history.
claims.claim: It was probably the worst administration in history.

claims.text: There's never been.
claims.claim: There's never been.

claims.text: And as far as Afghanistan is concerned, I was getting out of Afghanistan, but we were getting out with dignity, with strength, with power.
claims.claim: I was getting out of Afghanistan with dignity, strength, power
claims.correction: U.S. withdrawal occurred under Biden in August 2021 with 13 U.S. deaths during evacuation

claims.text: He got out.
claims.claim: He got out.

claims.text: It was the most embarrassing day in the history of our country's life.
claims.claim: It was the most embarrassing day in the history of our country's life.
claims.source: Joint Committee on Taxation / Committee for a Responsible Federal Budget

claims.text: President Trump, over the last eight years, under both of your administrations, the national debt soared to record highs.
claims.claim: National debt soared to record highs under Trump and Biden administrations
claims.correction: Debt rose about $7.8 trillion under Trump; record deficits mainly in 2020 due to COVID relief

claims.text: And according to a new nonpartisan analysis, President Trump, your administration approved $8.4 trillion in new debt, while so far, President Biden, you've approved $4.3 trillion in new debt.
claims.claim: Trump approved $8.4T new debt; Biden approved $4.3T new debt
claims.correction: Trump's term saw about $7.8T new debt; Biden's new debt not specified in references

claims.text: So former President Trump, many of the tax cuts that you signed into law are set to expire next year.
claims.claim: Many tax cuts Trump signed are set to expire next year
claims.source: Joint Committee on Taxation / Committee for a Responsible Federal Budget

claims.text: You want to extend them and go even further, you say, with the U.S. facing trillion dollar deficits and record debt, why should top earners and corporations pay even less in taxes than they do now?
claims.claim: Top earners and corporations pay less in taxes despite trillion dollar deficits

claims.text: Because the tax cuts spurred the greatest economy that we've ever seen just prior to COVID and even after COVID.
claims.claim: Tax cuts spurred the greatest economy ever before and after COVID
claims.correction: 2017 tax cuts were not the largest; pre-COVID GDP growth was solid but not historic
claims.source: Joint Committee on Taxation / Committee for a Responsible Federal Budget

claims.text: It was so strong that we were able to get through COVID much better than just about any other country.
claims.claim: We got through COVID much better than almost any other country
claims.correction: Over 400,000 Americans died by Jan 2021; U.S. COVID response faced criticism

claims.text: But we spurred that tax spurred.
claims.claim: We spurred that tax spurred
claims.source: Joint Committee on Taxation / Committee for a Responsible Federal Budget

claims.text: Now, when we cut the taxes, as an example, the corporate tax was cut down to 21 percent from 39 percent, plus beyond that.
claims.claim: Corporate tax was cut to 21% from 39%
claims.source: Joint Committee on Taxation / Committee for a Responsible Federal Budget

claims.text: We took in more revenue with much less tax, and companies were bringing back trillions of dollars back into our country.
claims.claim: More revenue with much less tax and trillions repatriated by companies
claims.correction: 2017 tax cuts reduced revenue by about $1.5 trillion over 10 years; no evidence of trillions repatriated
claims.source: Joint Committee on Taxation / Committee for a Responsible Federal Budget

claims.text: The country was going like never before, and we were ready to start paying down debt.
claims.claim: The country was going like never before and ready to pay down debt
claims.correction: GDP growth was solid but not unprecedented; debt and deficits rose substantially under Trump

claims.text: We're ready to start using the liquid gold right under our feet, the oil and gas right under our feet.
claims.claim: Ready to start using the oil and gas under our feet

claims.text: We were going to have something that nobody else has had.
claims.claim: We were going to have something that nobody else has had.

claims.text: We got hit with COVID.
claims.claim: We got hit with COVID
claims.source: U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

claims.text: We did a lot to fix it.
claims.claim: We did a lot to fix it.

claims.text: I gave him an unbelievable situation with all of the therapeutics and all of the things that we came up with.
claims.claim: I gave him an unbelievable situation with therapeutics and things we came up with

claims.text: We gave him something great.
claims.claim: We gave him something great.

claims.text: Remember, more people died under his administration, even though we had largely fixed it.
claims.claim: More people died under his administration despite prior fixes
claims.correction: Over 400,000 Americans died of COVID-19 by January 2021 under Trump
claims.source: U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

claims.text: More people died under his administration than our administration, and we were right in the middle of it, something which a lot of people don't like to talk about.
claims.claim: More people died under his administration than our administration
claims.source: U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

claims.text: But he had far more people dying in his administration.
claims.claim: More people died during his administration
claims.correction: Over 400,000 Americans died of COVID-19 by January 2021 under Trump
claims.source: U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

claims.text: He did the mandate, which is a disaster, mandating it.
claims.claim: He did the mandate, which is a disaster, mandating it

claims.text: The vaccine went out.
claims.claim: The vaccine went out
claims.source: U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

claims.text: He did a mandate on the vaccine, which is the thing that people most objected to about the vaccine.
claims.claim: He did a mandate on the vaccine
claims.correction: No reference states Trump implemented a COVID-19 vaccine mandate

claims.text: And he did a very poor job, just a very poor job.
claims.claim: He did a very poor job

claims.text: And I will tell you, not only poor there, but throughout the entire world we're no longer respected as a country.
claims.claim: The United States is no longer respected as a country worldwide

claims.text: They don't respect our leadership.
claims.claim: They don't respect our leadership.

claims.text: They don't respect the United States anymore.
claims.claim: They don't respect the United States anymore.

claims.text: We're like a third world nation between weaponization of his election, trying to go after his political opponent.
claims.claim: We're like a third world nation between weaponization of his election

claims.text: All of the things he's done, we've become like a third world nation, and it's a shame.
claims.claim: We've become like a third world nation under his actions
claims.correction: Economy lost 2.7 million jobs; debt rose $7.8 trillion; COVID deaths 400,000+ by 2021

claims.text: The damage he's done to our country, and I'd love to ask him, and will, why he allowed millions of people to come in here from prisons, jails, and mental institutions to come into our country and destroy our country.
claims.claim: Millions entered the country from prisons, jails, and mental institutions
claims.source: U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

claims.text: President Trump, we will get to immigration later in this block.
claims.claim: President Trump, we will get to immigration later in this block

claims.text: President Biden, I want to give you an opportunity to respond to this question about the national debt.
claims.claim: President Biden is given opportunity to respond about national debt

claims.text: He had the largest national debt of any president in a four-year period, number one.
claims.claim: Trump had the largest national debt increase in a four-year period

claims.text: Number two, he got two trillion-dollar tax card benefited the very wealthy.
claims.claim: He got two trillion-dollar tax cuts benefiting the very wealthy
claims.correction: The 2017 Tax Cuts and Jobs Act cut taxes by about $1.5 trillion over ten years, not $2 trillion

claims.text: When I'm going to do a fix the taxes, for example, we have 1,000 trillionaires in America, I mean, billionaires in America.
claims.claim: There are 1,000 billionaires in America
claims.correction: The references do not support the claim of 1,000 billionaires in America; no billionaire count is provided.
claims.source: Joint Committee on Taxation / Committee for a Responsible Federal Budget

claims.text: And what's happening?
claims.claim: And what's happening?

claims.text: They're in a situation where they, in fact, pay 8.2% in taxes.
claims.claim: They pay 8.2% in taxes
claims.source: Joint Committee on Taxation / Committee for a Responsible Federal Budget

claims.text: If they just paid 24%, 25%, either one of those numbers, they've raised $500 million, billion, I should say, in a 10-year period.
claims.claim: Paying 24% or 25% tax raised $500 billion in 10 years

claims.text: We'd be able to right wipe out his debt.
claims.claim: We'd be able to right wipe out his debt
claims.correction: Reference facts do not provide information on wiping out debt

claims.text: We'd be able to help make sure that all those things we need to do, child care, elder care, making sure that we continue to strengthen our health care system, making sure that we're able to make every single solitary person eligible for what I've been able to do with
claims.claim: Help with child care, elder care, health care, and eligibility for benefits
claims.source: U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

claims.text: the COVID.
claims.claim: the COVID
claims.source: U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

claims.text: I'm just going to be dealing with everything we have to do with...
claims.claim: I'm just going to be dealing with everything we have to do with
